notes: Two-year fellowship supporting early-career researchers studying CFTR biology and cystic fibrosis-related basic science.

name: Research Fellowship Award
deadline: Annual (recent cycle: LOI December, full application February)
amount: Up to $70,000/year (salary match up to $50,000 plus fringe up to $12,500 and $7,500 for non-salary project costs)
other_benefits: Funds available for scientific meeting travel and professional membership dues
eligibility: Postdoctoral researchers employed by a US health-related institution developing basic or clinical research skills in inflammatory bowel disease (Crohn's disease or ulcerative colitis)
notes: 1-3 year postdoctoral award preparing researchers for independent careers in IBD research; open regardless of citizenship provided work is based at a US institution.

name: Research Fellowship for All (AARFA)
amount: Up to $200,000 over up to 3 years
other_benefits: $10,000 completion stipend to the fellow and $10,000 to the mentor upon successful completion of the award
eligibility: Postdoctoral researchers with a doctoral degree, within 10 years of terminal degree, full-time position at a nonprofit academic/medical/research institution, not yet holding independent faculty appointment
notes: Broadened successor to the original AARF program; funds dementia and Alzheimer's-related research training for early-career scientists.

name: Postdoctoral Fellowship
amount: Salary stipend (scaled by experience) plus $7,000/year fringe and $8,000/year training allowance; up to $294,084 total over 3 years
other_benefits: Training allowance supports professional development activities
eligibility: Postdoctoral researchers conducting diabetes-related research at US nonprofit institutions
notes: Supports up to three years of postdoctoral training focused on diabetes research.

name: Postdoctoral Fellowship (Individual Biomedical Research Award)
opens: By institutional nomination (Foundation designates Top Ten Centers annually)
other_benefits: Funds may cover salary, health insurance, or scientific-meeting travel
eligibility: US citizens/permanent residents holding a PhD or equivalent in early-stage research career, nominated internally by one of the Foundation's Top Ten Centers for Biomedical Research
flags: Institutional nomination required; only available at Hartwell-designated Top Ten Center institutions, not an open application
notes: Funds applied, early-stage biomedical research intended to benefit children in the US, awarded through a competitive internal institutional nomination process rather than direct individual application.

name: Postdoctoral Physician Scientist Fellowship
eligibility: MD or MD/PhD enrolled in or having completed an ACGME-accredited rheumatology fellowship, within 5 years of doctoral degree, affiliated with a US nonprofit research institution
notes: Supports postdoctoral physician-scientists studying arthritis and related autoimmune diseases such as lupus, scleroderma, and psoriatic arthritis.

organization: American Federation for Aging Research (AFAR) / Glenn Foundation for Medical Research
name: Postdoctoral Fellowship in Aging Research
other_benefits: Access to AFAR mentoring network and aging-research community
eligibility: Postdoctoral fellows (MD/PhD or equivalent) conducting basic or translational aging research at a US nonprofit institution
notes: One-year award supporting up to 12 postdoctoral fellows annually researching the basic biology of aging and its translational/clinical applications.

name: Career Transition Award (CTA)
amount: Up to $650,000 over up to 5 years (Phase 1: up to $100,000/year for up to 2 years of postdoctoral work; Phase 2: up to $150,000/year for up to 3 years as an independent investigator)
other_benefits: Structured two-phase mentored transition support from postdoctoral training into an independent tenure-track research career
eligibility: Postdoctoral and clinical fellows in the final years of mentored training pursuing breast cancer research, based at a US nonprofit institution, transitioning to independent faculty positions
notes: Two-phase award bridging mentored postdoctoral research and early independent faculty research specifically in breast cancer; open regardless of citizenship provided the research is based at a US institution.

name: Junior Fellowship in Byzantine, Garden & Landscape, or Pre-Columbian Studies
amount: $25,000 (Junior Fellow, PhD candidate) or $35,000 (Fellow, recent PhD), per academic year
other_benefits: Campus housing or housing allowance, weekday lunch, health insurance option, on-site library/museum/collections access, resident scholarly community
eligibility: PhD candidates (ABD, all preliminary requirements completed) or recent PhDs, in Byzantine Studies, Garden and Landscape Studies, or Pre-Columbian Studies
notes: Harvard-administered residential fellowship spanning three distinct humanities fields, each with dedicated library and collections at the Dumbarton Oaks campus in Washington, DC; single application portal covers all three fields.

name: School of Historical Studies Membership / Postdoctoral Fellowship
amount: Up to $80,000 (full year) or $40,000 (single term); dedicated Postdoctoral Fellowship pays $75,000/year
other_benefits: Office space, library access, subsidized dining and housing, administrative support, resident community of scholars
eligibility: PhD in hand; early-career scholars specifically eligible via the dedicated Postdoctoral Fellowship (Medieval Studies; Europe and the World post-1800) and a Mellon Fellowship for current assistant professors, plus reduced reference requirements for PhDs earned in the last ~6 years
notes: General Membership is open to scholars at all career stages, but dedicated early-career sub-tracks (Postdoctoral Fellowship, Mellon Fellowship) specifically target recent PhDs and junior faculty.

name: Short-Term Fellowship
other_benefits: Access to the Newberry's specialized research centers and collections (Renaissance studies, American Indian and Indigenous Studies, cartography); colloquia presentations
eligibility: ABD PhD candidates, postdoctoral scholars, faculty, and holders of terminal non-PhD degrees (MFA/MLIS/MSW/JD)
notes: Broad eligibility explicitly includes ABD graduate students; funds 1-2 month research visits to the Newberry's humanities collections in Chicago.

name: Long-Term Fellowship
other_benefits: Full immersion in Newberry collections and community of fellows; works-in-progress presentations
eligibility: PhD (or equivalent terminal degree) in hand; postdoctoral scholars (ABD not eligible)
notes: 4-9 month residential fellowship for postdoctoral humanities scholars; a distinct, postdoc-only tier from the Newberry's ABD-eligible Short-Term Fellowship.

name: Short-Term Fellowship
other_benefits: Residency access to AAS library and collections in Worcester, MA; priority campus housing
eligibility: ABD doctoral candidates, postdoctoral scholars, independent scholars, and faculty
flags: Cannot sponsor J-1 visas
notes: Most of the 19 named short-term awards specifically target dissertation research by ABD graduate students in early American history and culture.

name: Hench Post-Dissertation Fellowship
other_benefits: Full-time residential access to AAS collections; on-campus housing option; resident fellow community
eligibility: Postdoctoral scholars within 3 years of receiving their PhD (ABD not eligible)
flags: Cannot sponsor J-1 visas
notes: 12-month early-career postdoctoral fellowship; became the Society's only live long-term fellowship after its AAS-NEH Long-Term Fellowship was terminated in 2025 federal funding cuts.

name: Dissertation Fellowship
other_benefits: Mandatory Winter Seminar (travel and lodging covered) with a cross-program cohort of fellows
eligibility: ABD PhD/ThD candidates at accredited North American schools researching American religion, expecting to complete within ~2 years; US/Canada residency
flags: Topic restricted to Christian faith/ministry/religious institutions or religion and social issues in North America
notes: Funds the final dissertation-writing year for approximately 10 doctoral candidates per year studying American religious life.

organization: Cornell University, Society for the Humanities
name: Society for the Humanities Fellowship
other_benefits: Office and community space at the Society; teaches one seminar (2 hrs/week, up to 15 students); weekly Fellows Seminar and luncheon
eligibility: Postdoctoral scholars in any humanities field tied to the year's theme; PhD must be completed before the fellowship start date (no ABD); no citizenship restriction, visa-eligible internationals welcome
notes: One-year residential, thematic postdoctoral fellowship (2027-28 theme: 'PLAY'); interdisciplinary cohort organized around a single humanities theme each year.

name: NEH Fellowships
deadline: Apr 2027 (est.; most recent confirmed cycle deadline was Apr 22, 2026)
other_benefits: Dedicated full-time research and writing time; funds paid directly to the individual scholar rather than an institution
eligibility: Scholars who have completed all degree requirements for a doctorate except conferral of the degree, through senior scholars; not for currently enrolled degree-seeking students
flags: 2026 competition limited to projects on American history and culture or Western civilization; program underwent 2025 federal grant terminations later ruled unlawful in court and is being reinstated — verify current cycle status before applying
notes: Flagship federal humanities fellowship funding 6-12 months of full-time research and writing for near-PhD and established scholars; experienced significant funding disruption in 2025-2026, so confirm active status before relying on it.

organization: Consortium for Graduate Study in Management (The Consortium/CGSM)
name: Consortium MBA Fellowship
other_benefits: Full-tuition merit fellowship at admitting member school (not guaranteed for all admits); access to The Consortium's 5-day Orientation Program and Career Forum with 1,200+ MBA prospects, recruiters, and alumni; ongoing corporate networking and career-services membership benefits.
eligibility: US citizens/permanent residents (some member schools also admit qualified international applicants) applying to full-time MBA programs at one of 20+ Consortium member business schools, with demonstrated commitment to advancing diversity and inclusion in business education and corporate leadership.
notes: One of the longest-running diversity-in-business MBA fellowship networks (est. 1966); over 400 full-tuition fellowships awarded annually across member schools, though not every admitted Consortium member receives fellowship funding.

name: Forté MBA Fellows Program
deadline: Varies by member school's standard MBA admissions deadlines
other_benefits: MBA Virtual Leadership Conference, Financial Services Fast Track, lifetime access to online career-development programming, inclusion in the Forté Fellows resume book distributed to member companies, and a mentoring program.
eligibility: MBA degree candidates (full-time, part-time, executive, dual-degree, or online) admitted to a Forté member business school, showing leadership and a commitment to advancing women in business; open to all nationalities and gender identities.
flags: Focused on advancing women in business (open to all gender identities)
notes: Over $595 million awarded to roughly 24,000 Fellows since inception, confirmed on the official program page; individual award amounts are set independently by each of the 50+ partner business schools rather than paid directly by the Foundation.

name: ROMBA Fellowship
opens: Rolling (Fall MBA admissions cycle, via partner school)
deadline: Varies by school (typically up to Round 3 admissions deadline or until fellows are selected)
other_benefits: Multi-day Fellowship Retreat with lodging covered, free admission to ROMBA events during MBA studies, summer treks with LGBTQ+ industry leaders, inclusion in a dedicated Fellows resume book for corporate partners, and professional development webinars/regional events.
eligibility: MBA applicants admitted to a Reaching Out partner business school who demonstrate LGBTQ+ community leadership and/or allyship; each partner school independently selects its own Fellows.
flags: LGBTQ+ (and demonstrated allies) focus
notes: Fellows serve as campus ambassadors supporting LGBTQ+ initiatives and recruitment; the annual ROMBA Conference is the largest LGBTQ+ MBA gathering in the world.

name: Dissertation Fellows Program
other_benefits: Paid in-residence 12-week placement at the Board in Washington, D.C.; mentorship/collaboration with Board economists in Financial Stability, International Finance, Monetary Affairs, or Research and Statistics; fellows deliver two research seminars during the fellowship.
eligibility: PhD students in economics or finance, typically 4th- or 5th-year candidates, who are legally authorized to work in the US.
notes: Paid summer in-residence fellowship (roughly 12-17 awarded annually) for advanced PhD candidates in economics/finance to further dissertation research on-site at the Federal Reserve Board.

name: NAIC MBA Fellowship Program
deadline: Annual (typically early spring; confirm current-cycle date on website)
other_benefits: In-person NAIC meetings/events with travel expenses covered; direct exposure to diverse-owned alternative investment and private equity firms; mentorship and networking in alternative investments.
eligibility: Second-year MBA students focused on finance, from any US business school, aspiring to careers in alternative investments/private equity.
flags: Second-year MBA students only; mostly remote with occasional required travel
notes: Introduces diverse second-year MBA students to diverse-owned alternative investment firms via a paid, ~10-month, mostly-remote fellowship.

name: Mosaic Fellowship
other_benefits: Mandatory NYC orientation with costs reimbursed by ICM; placement as a Summer Associate at an ICM member impact-investing 'Host Fund'; some host funds add travel/relocation stipends or bonuses; mentorship and network access.
eligibility: Graduate students currently enrolled in any degree program (MBA not required); ICM actively welcomes applicants from underrepresented and diverse backgrounds.
flags: Full-time 10-week summer commitment required (June-August); ~21 fellows selected per cycle across ~20 host funds
notes: Flagship paid summer fellowship placing graduate students as Summer Associates at impact-investing/private capital funds; has supported 115+ fellows since 2019.

name: Halcyon US Health Accelerator (Fellowship)
other_benefits: Two one-week in-person residencies (Chicago and Washington DC), mentorship and business advising, no equity taken.
eligibility: Early-stage, US-based health-focused venture founders/CEOs with a working MVP, age 21+, English fluency.
flags: US-based ventures only; health vertical only
notes: Formerly the DC residential 'Halcyon Incubator' fellowship; the organization rebranded as Halcyon Accelerator and now runs vertical-specific cohort programs, of which the US Health Accelerator is the current cycle.

name: Camelback Fellowship
deadline: Varies by cycle — check website for current window
other_benefits: Dedicated 1:1 executive coach, investor/strategic-partner connections, peer community, curriculum on strategy, pitching, and team building.
eligibility: Founder/Co-founder/CEO/Executive Director of an early-stage venture in education or conscious technology; must reside in the US or a US territory, age 18+.
flags: Priority for entrepreneurs of color and women/non-binary leaders
notes: 16-week hybrid fellowship held twice a year; 94% of past Fellows identify as Black, Indigenous, or people of color, and 63% identify as women/non-binary.

organization: MIT Kuo Sharper Center for Prosperity and Entrepreneurship (formerly the Legatum Center)
name: Legatum Fellowship (MIT Student Fellowship)
deadline: See application portal for current-cycle deadline
other_benefits: Impact Ventures entrepreneurial seminar, Entrepreneur-in-Residence mentorship, travel/prototyping grants, semester pitch events.
eligibility: Current or incoming MIT graduate (and some undergraduate) students building ventures in emerging markets.
flags: MIT students only
notes: Originally the Legatum Center Fellowship; MIT Sloan's entrepreneurship center was renamed the Kuo Sharper Center and the fellowship continues under that name.

organization: Stanford Graduate School of Business (Center for Social Innovation)
name: Stanford Impact Founder (SIF) Fellowship
deadline: February (Annual) — check website for current-cycle date
other_benefits: Year-long personalized impact and entrepreneurship advising; automatic invitation to the Stanford Venture Studio resident program.
eligibility: Graduating Stanford MBA/MSx students (Social Entrepreneurship track, GSB only) or graduating graduate students from any Stanford school (Ecopreneurship track).
flags: Stanford graduate students only
notes: One of the largest stipends among social entrepreneurship fellowships nationally, confirmed active via Stanford's own application portal.

name: Social Entrepreneurship Fellowship
deadline: April 1 (Annual) — check website for current-cycle date
amount: Up to $65,000 (bridges salary to $130,000 total compensation over 12 months)
other_benefits: Two-installment disbursement, quarterly touchpoints with HBS staff/faculty, can be combined with certain HBS loan-assistance programs.
eligibility: Graduating HBS MBA students who are founders of a social enterprise (nonprofit, for-profit, or hybrid), committing full-time (35+ hrs/week) for 12+ months, venture must have raised under $1.5M.
flags: Harvard MBA students only; one Fellow selected per year
notes: Highly selective — only one student is chosen annually; funding is disbursed in two equal payments across the fellowship year.

name: Entrepreneurial Fellows Program
deadline: Rolling — check website for current window
amount: $80,000 annual salary plus benefits, plus a $10,000 stipend for supplies and travel
other_benefits: One year partnering with Northwestern faculty to develop and commercialize a Northwestern-originated technology toward launching a startup; direct faculty mentorship.
eligibility: Recent PhD or MBA graduates (degree earned within 5 years of program start); open to graduates of any university, requires a written endorsement letter from a Northwestern faculty PI-innovator prior to applying.
flags: Requires securing a Northwestern faculty endorsement before applying; project must center on commercializing a specific Northwestern technology
notes: One-year, salaried entrepreneurship fellowship for recent PhD/MBA grads who want to launch a startup around a Northwestern faculty innovation.

name: Fellowship for Accounting Doctoral Students
other_benefits: Renewable annually for up to three consecutive years upon proof of enrollment and program progress; positions recipients to become accounting faculty and mentors at US universities.
eligibility: Licensed CPAs who have applied to or matriculated in a full-time AACSB-accredited accounting PhD or DBA program in the US, intend to teach/research at a US university after graduation, and have at least one year remaining in their program.
flags: Must be a licensed CPA; US citizens/permanent residents only
notes: Administered by the AICPA Foundation to build the pipeline of accounting faculty; awards multiple $15,000 fellowships annually, renewable for up to three years with proof of program progress.

name: Skadden Fellowship
amount: $68,000/year base salary support (plus employer FICA and supplemental funding for higher-paying hosts)
other_benefits: Two-year fellowship; annual symposium in New York; complimentary LexisNexis access and CLE via PLI; pro bono support from Skadden attorneys; ongoing mentoring and alumni network
eligibility: Final-year law students, LL.M. candidates, current judicial clerks, or law grads within the prior two years, authorized to work in the US
notes: Long-running, well-known public interest law fellowship funding self-designed projects at nonprofit/public interest host organizations for two years.

name: Design-Your-Own Fellowship
amount: Up to $66,500/year in salary support to host organization
other_benefits: Two-year fellowship; host org also provides health insurance and fringe benefits; fellow co-designs their own public interest legal project
eligibility: Law school graduates/JDs partnering with a US nonprofit host organization to co-design a two-year public interest legal project
notes: Major national public interest law fellowship program; current cycle (fellows starting fall 2027) is open now, window May 21-Sep 17, 2026.

name: The Honorable Phyllis W. Beck Public Interest Law Fellowships
amount: $70,000 (year 1) / $72,000 (year 2) minimum salary; up to $10,000/year in loan repayment assistance
other_benefits: Foundation reimburses sponsoring organization for health/disability insurance and employer payroll taxes; renewable for a second year
eligibility: Law grads, outgoing judicial clerks, or attorneys within five years of law school graduation, with a sponsoring public interest legal organization
flags: Sponsoring organization must be located in Philadelphia, Delaware, Montgomery, Bucks, or Chester County, Pennsylvania
notes: One-year (renewable) fellowship funding direct legal representation of disadvantaged clients in the Philadelphia region.

name: Justice Catalyst Fellowship
amount: $75,000/year in salary support paid to host organization
other_benefits: Mentorship during optional prospectus phase; possible 3-12 month project extension upon renewal
eligibility: Graduating law students or law graduates up to two years out of law school, matched with a nonprofit, union, government agency, or public-interest law firm host
notes: One-year, potentially renewable, project-based legal fellowship for innovative public interest work.

name: Justice Fellowship
amount: Minimum $62,500/year salary plus benefits (actual salary may be higher depending on host organization)
other_benefits: Health insurance; at least three weeks paid time off; training and professional development; placement with partner host organizations; alumni network
eligibility: JD or LL.M. graduates within two years of law school graduation, authorized to work in the US for the full two-year term
flags: Immigration law focus; requires US work authorization for full 2-year term
notes: Two-year fellowship placing law graduates at immigration legal service organizations nationwide; over 90% of alumni continue in immigration law.

organization: Reporters Committee for Freedom of the Press
name: Media Litigation Fellowship
other_benefits: Fully paid health insurance, paid time off, 401(k); hands-on litigation experience with RCFP staff attorneys on First Amendment and free press matters
eligibility: Recent law graduates/early-career lawyers with First Amendment interest, admitted to at least one US bar
notes: Two-year litigation fellowship at RCFP's DC office; part of a family of RCFP legal fellowships including a 1-year Legal Fellowship and the Jack Nelson-Dow Jones Fellowship.

name: Post-Law School Legal Fellowship
amount: $70,000/year salary (fellow must also secure minimum $45,000 in external funding)
other_benefits: Full benefits; litigation experience on EFF's docket in its San Francisco office
eligibility: Recent law graduates who will sit for/pass a bar exam and can secure at least $45,000 in external funding; US work authorization required
flags: Requires applicant to secure own external funding source; must relocate to San Francisco
notes: EFF hosts up to two post-law-school litigation fellows per year working on digital rights and civil liberties litigation.

organization: Yale Law School Information Society Project
name: ISP Resident Fellowship
amount: $70,000/year plus Yale benefits and travel stipend
other_benefits: Access to Yale University resources; participation in ISP scholarly community and programming; renewable for a second year
eligibility: Recent JD, PhD, or equivalent degree holders pursuing an academic career, with research aligned to ISP focus areas (currently AI and the digital public sphere)
notes: Tech-law academic fellowship; fellows must be in New Haven at least 3 days/week and produce at least two scholarly works annually.

name: Justice Fellowship (formerly Bertha Justice Fellowship)
deadline: Annual (recent cycle closed Oct 2025; next cohort expected ~2027)
other_benefits: 401(k) employer contributions; 100% employer-paid health, dental, life, and disability insurance; paid vacation, sick leave, and holidays
eligibility: J.D. or equivalent LL.M., early-career lawyers 0-2 years post-degree, committed to social justice litigation
notes: Formerly the Bertha Justice Fellowship; a 2-year salaried fellowship placing early-career lawyers in CCR's social-justice litigation and movement-lawyering work.

name: Schreiber Legal Fellowship
other_benefits: Health benefits; hybrid work model; placement at an NIJC office in Chicago, Indiana, San Diego, or Washington, DC
eligibility: JD from an ABA-accredited law school; current 3Ls or attorneys who graduated within the last three years, bar member or sitting for the bar before start
notes: Two-year fellowship providing direct legal representation to immigrants, refugees, and asylum seekers; recurring program with prior cohorts.

name: Ann Kolker Fellowship
other_benefits: Placement on NWLC's Reproductive Rights and Health team in Washington, DC; full employee benefits package
eligibility: Early-career lawyer (JD required) interested in health equity, reproductive rights, and health policy work
flags: One-year term-limited position, renewed based on funding availability each cycle
notes: Recurring one-year fellowship honoring health advocate Ann Kolker; applications accepted on a rolling basis until filled.

organization: Judge David L. Bazelon Center for Mental Health Law
name: Bazelon Fellowship in Mental Health Law
other_benefits: Generous benefits; two years of litigation and policy experience on disability civil rights cases; based in Washington, DC
eligibility: Georgetown University Law Center graduates or current students graduating by May of the fellowship's start year
flags: Restricted to Georgetown University Law Center graduates/students only
notes: Two-year, $60,000/year paid fellowship; fellow works on civil rights litigation and policy for people with mental disabilities.

name: Tyron Garner Memorial Fellowship for African-American LGBT Civil Rights
opens: Periodic (recruited as openings arise — see lambdalegal.org/jobs-and-internships for current postings)
other_benefits: Mentorship from Lambda Legal attorneys; hands-on impact litigation, policy advocacy, and public education work
eligibility: Recent law graduates, current fellows/clerks, or early-career attorneys dedicated to LGBTQ+ issues within African American communities, bar-eligible
notes: Established 2015 in memory of the Lawrence v. Texas plaintiff; a 2-year commitment fellowship recruited periodically rather than on a fixed annual calendar.

name: ACS Congressional Fellowship
other_benefits: Health insurance reimbursement, relocation and travel allowances, two-week AAAS orientation on federal policymaking, placement as full-time staff on Capitol Hill
eligibility: PhD chemists/chemical professionals (or equivalent experience), ACS members, US citizens
flags: ACS membership required
notes: One of the AAAS-network Science & Technology Policy Fellowships; places a PhD chemist as full-time staff in a congressional office or committee for one year.

name: APS Congressional Science Fellowship
amount: $91,000 stipend plus relocation, travel, and health insurance allowances
other_benefits: Placement in a congressional office/committee, AAAS orientation program, professional development
eligibility: PhD in physics or related field (or master's plus 3 years' experience), APS member, US citizen
flags: APS membership required
notes: Places one APS member as a full-time science advisor in a congressional office or committee for one year; part of the AAAS Science & Technology Policy Fellowships network.

name: AGU Congressional Science Fellowship
other_benefits: Travel allowances, AGU Annual Meeting registration, professional development
eligibility: PhD geoscientists/related fields, US citizens or permanent residents
notes: Places a geoscientist with a doctoral degree in a congressional office or committee for one year to bring scientific expertise to policymaking.

name: IEEE-USA Congressional Fellowship
other_benefits: Placement in a Capitol Hill office, orientation, networking with fellow policy fellows
eligibility: US citizen, IEEE member, PhD (or in final stages) or master's plus experience in an engineering/technical field, must reside in DC metro area
flags: IEEE membership required
notes: One-year placement of a technically trained IEEE member on Capitol Hill; includes a newer Congressional Electric Grid Policy Fellowship track.

name: APA Congressional Fellowship
other_benefits: $3,500 professional development stipend, health insurance reimbursement, two-week orientation program, yearlong policy seminar series
eligibility: Full APA members (PhD in psychology), US citizens
flags: APA membership required
notes: One-year placement of a psychologist as full-time staff to a member of Congress or a congressional committee.

organization: Society for Research in Child Development
name: SRCD U.S. Policy Fellowship Program
amount: ~$101,400 stipend (federal GS-12 scale, based on post-PhD experience)
other_benefits: $2,800 professional development fund, moving allowance up to $1,300, health insurance allowance
eligibility: Doctoral degree (PhD, EdD, MD) in a child-development-relevant field, SRCD member
flags: SRCD membership required
notes: One-year congressional or state-legislature placements applying child-development research to policy; federal executive-branch placements are currently paused, but congressional/state tracks remain active.

name: Commonwealth Fund Fellowship in Minority Health Policy at Harvard University
other_benefits: Full tuition, individual health insurance, books, travel, and financial assistance for a practicum project; leads to an MPH or MPA degree
eligibility: Physicians (MD) early in career committed to addressing health disparities
flags: Physicians (MD) only
notes: One-year, degree-granting fellowship at Harvard preparing physicians to become health-equity policy leaders.

name: Rotary Peace Fellowship
other_benefits: Full tuition and fees, monthly stipend for room/board, round-trip transportation, internship/field-study funding, contingency fund
eligibility: Bachelor's degree; 3+ years relevant work experience (master's track) or 5+ years (certificate track); not currently enrolled in a degree program
notes: Funds a master's degree or professional certificate in peace and conflict studies at one of Rotary's Peace Centers worldwide; open to US and international applicants.

name: Luce Scholars Program
other_benefits: Language training, individualized professional placement in Asia, program orientation
eligibility: US citizens/permanent residents under 33, college seniors through young professionals, limited prior Asia experience
notes: 13-month program placing young American professionals in individualized fellowships across Asia; not tied to a specific field of study.

name: Toigo MBA Fellowship
amount: $5,000 fellowship award, renewable one additional year
other_benefits: Structured mentorship, career development, finance-industry network, Fellowship-in-Residence conference
eligibility: Underrepresented-minority students entering/in a 2-year MBA (or JD/MBA, MSF) program pursuing finance careers; US citizens, permanent residents, or authorized to work in the US
flags: Underrepresented minorities in finance
notes: Combines a financial award with structured mentorship and a required post-graduation commitment to a finance career.

name: Optica/MRS Congressional Science and Engineering Fellowship
other_benefits: Health insurance, travel and relocation expenses to the Washington, DC area, AAAS orientation program, yearlong policy seminar series
eligibility: PhD in optical science/technology or materials science by fellowship start, member of Optica or MRS, authorized to work in the US
flags: Optica or MRS membership required
notes: Co-sponsored one-year fellowship placing an optics/materials scientist as a legislative assistant for a member of Congress or a committee.

name: Getty Postdoctoral Fellowship in the History of Art
other_benefits: Workstation at the Getty Research Institute or Getty Villa, relocation travel stipend, scholar housing, healthcare option
eligibility: Received PhD within the past 5 years, working on an art-history-related research project
notes: Year-long residential fellowship at the Getty in Los Angeles for early-career art historians to pursue original research.

name: Glenn T. Seaborg Congressional Science and Engineering Fellowship
amount: $95,000 stipend plus up to $10,000 travel reimbursement
other_benefits: AAAS orientation program, placement as legislative staff on Capitol Hill
eligibility: PhD in applicable science/engineering field with nuclear-related discipline and 1+ years' experience, or master's in engineering with 3 years' professional experience
notes: ANS sponsors up to two Fellows per year for one-year placements as full-time congressional staff bringing nuclear science expertise to policymaking.

organization: Charles Hamilton Houston Institute for Race & Justice (Harvard Law School)
name: Guinier Project Postdoctoral Fellowship
other_benefits: Standard Harvard postdoctoral benefits, mentorship, research support
eligibility: Early-career scholars (JD/PhD) with research interests in racial representation, democracy, voting rights, or electoral reform
notes: One-year (renewable up to two additional years) postdoctoral fellowship at Harvard Law focused on voting rights and electoral reform scholarship.

name: APS Library & Museum Short-Term Resident Research Fellowship
other_benefits: Access to APS Library & Museum archival collections in Philadelphia
eligibility: PhD candidates (ABD), PhD holders, and degreed independent scholars in any field
notes: Short-term residential research fellowship supporting archival research at the APS Library & Museum in Philadelphia; open to doctoral candidates and beyond.

name: Local Government Management Fellowship (LGMF)
other_benefits: Two years complimentary ICMA membership, free annual conference registration, scholarship support for Local Government Reimagined conferences, management assessment access, PM magazine subscription
eligibility: Recent graduates or students completing an MPA/MPP or related master's degree from a NASPAA-accredited program by August 31 of the fellowship year; must be authorized to work in the US
flags: Requires NASPAA-accredited master's degree; no prior local-government-management experience allowed
notes: 12-month paid fellowship placing recent MPA/MPP graduates in full-time local government management positions with a host city or county; over 425 fellows placed across 20 years, many advancing to senior local-government leadership.

name: New York State Senate Graduate Fellowship Program
other_benefits: Employee benefits during the fellowship term; direct placement in legislative offices in Albany
eligibility: Completed graduate studies at an accredited university prior to the fellowship start
flags: Albany, NY-based; fellows must secure their own housing; graduate degree must be completed before fellowship starts
notes: Established in 1965, this nine-month, full-time legislative fellowship places graduate/postgraduate fellows directly in New York State Senate offices doing policy research, drafting, and constituent work.

organization: National Security Education Program (NSEP) / Boren Awards
name: Boren Fellowship
deadline: Annual (national deadline typically January; 2027-2028 cycle date TBA)
amount: Up to $25,000 for 25-52 weeks, plus an optional $5,000 for summer domestic language study (shorter 12-24 week awards funded at $12,500)
other_benefits: Language and area-studies training in regions critical to US national security; access to federal career resources and hiring events
eligibility: US citizen graduate students matriculated in or applying to a US graduate degree program, studying languages/regions critical to national security
flags: US citizens only; requires approximately one-year federal service commitment after the fellowship
notes: Funds graduate-level language study and research abroad in exchange for a commitment to work in the federal government, explicitly intended as a pipeline into US national security and policy careers.

organization: Center for California Studies (Sacramento State) / California State Government
name: Capital Fellows Programs (Executive, Senate, Assembly, and Judicial Administration Fellowships)
deadline: Annual (check back September 2026 for exact date)
other_benefits: Medical, dental, and vision benefits; $875 one-time moving allowance; paid enrollment in six graduate units; Graduate Certificate in Applied Policy and Government upon completion
eligibility: Bachelor's degree holders and early-career applicants (no advanced degree required); open nationally to US applicants willing to relocate to Sacramento
flags: Must relocate to Sacramento, CA; placements are within California state government (Legislative, Executive, or Judicial branch)
notes: Four nationally recognized tracks (Senate, Assembly, Executive, Judicial) placing fellows as full-time paid state government employees for 10-11 months; the Assembly Fellowship, established in 1957, is the nation's oldest legislative fellowship program.

name: Summer Research Fellowship (US)
other_benefits: Travel, lodging, and meals covered for a required in-person week in Washington, DC or Berkeley, CA
eligibility: Law students, early-career researchers, and academics working on U.S. AI law and policy; must be able to work full-time remotely June-August with the in-person week
notes: Paid full-time summer research fellowship at the Institute for Law & AI (formerly housed under the Legal Priorities Project) focused on U.S. AI law and regulation; runs as a recurring annual seasonal program.

name: Westin Research Fellowship
other_benefits: Full employee benefits package (medical/dental/vision, 401k access); option to work remotely or on-site at IAPP headquarters in Portsmouth, NH
eligibility: U.S. citizens, permanent residents, or those eligible for U.S. work authorization; undergraduate degree required, graduate degree in law or a related field preferred
notes: One-year paid research fellowship pairing scholars with IAPP's Westin Research Center on privacy and AI governance research, publications, and policy initiatives; runs annually.

name: Postdoctoral Fellow, AI-Enabled Scams as a Systemic Security Challenge
other_benefits: Medical, dental, and vision insurance; fully remote position
eligibility: PhD required (completed before September 2026) in communication, media studies, information studies, sociology, or related field; must be authorized to work in the U.S. without sponsorship
flags: Postdoctoral (PhD required); US work authorization required without sponsorship; single funded position rather than a recurring named program
notes: Two-year (Sept 2026-May 2028) postdoctoral research fellowship in Data & Society's AI & Democracy program studying how AI is being used to supercharge scams, fraud, and hoaxes.

name: El Pomar Fellowship
other_benefits: Structured professional development curriculum, alumni network of 300+, cohort mentorship, informational interviews with current Fellows
eligibility: Recent college graduates and early-career professionals willing to relocate to Colorado Springs, CO for a 2-year full-time commitment
flags: Colorado-focused; requires relocation to Colorado Springs
notes: A 2-year, full-time paid fellowship at El Pomar Foundation combining hands-on grantmaking and program administration with a leadership-development curriculum for early-career professionals entering Colorado's philanthropic and nonprofit sector.

name: Leadership Academy Fellowship Program
amount: $60,000 for the 11-month fellowship term (paid hourly at $33.70/hr)
other_benefits: Medical, dental, and vision coverage; paid holidays and PTO; wellness and professional development resources; mentorship; policy advocacy and public-speaking experience
eligibility: Early-career professionals; if BA held, 1+ year related experience required (or 2+ years without a BA); must reside within commuting distance of Oakland, CA; no visa sponsorship offered
flags: Oakland/Bay Area-based; no visa sponsorship for international applicants
notes: An 11-month, paid fellowship placing 4-6 early-career professionals per year on Greenlining Institute policy teams to build racial- and economic-equity leadership and nonprofit advocacy skills.

name: CFLeads Fellowship Program
deadline: Annual, ~early March (e.g., Mar 7 for a recent cycle)
amount: $25,000 stipend covering up to 20 hours/week over three semesters
other_benefits: Remote work arrangement; exposure to community foundation strategy, communications, and network engagement; professional network building in philanthropy
eligibility: Graduate-level students interested in community foundations and philanthropic leadership
flags: Part-time (up to 20 hrs/week); must be an enrolled graduate student
notes: Part-time, paid fellowship placing graduate students inside CFLeads' network to build skills in community foundation leadership, strategy, and communications over three semesters.

name: Census Bureau Dissertation Fellowship Program
deadline: Annual (Winter Quarter, Jan-Mar, via applicant's university)
other_benefits: On-site collaboration with Census Bureau researchers, mentorship, and a Bureau seminar presentation of findings
eligibility: Doctoral candidates (U.S. citizens) at accredited U.S. institutions researching statistical theory/methodology, survey measurement methodology, or data science methodology
flags: US citizens only; nominated/submitted through applicant's university, not directly by individuals
notes: Awards institutional contracts (covering tuition, research, travel, and salary) to fund doctoral dissertation research directly relevant to federal statistical data science and survey methodology.

organization: Federal Reserve Bank of San Francisco
name: Thomas J. Sargent Dissertation Fellowship
other_benefits: Six weeks on-site at the SF Fed, mentorship from Fed research economists, seminars, and networking with Bank staff
eligibility: PhD candidates researching macroeconomics, international trade, financial economics, urban economics, or labor economics using quantitative/statistical data analysis
flags: PhD candidates in select economics subfields only
notes: Six-week paid summer dissertation fellowship giving PhD students hands-on collaboration with SF Fed economists on data-driven economic research.

name: FDA Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Fellowship (CDRH)
other_benefits: Monthly stipend, hands-on work at FDA's White Oak, MD facility, up to 14 positions available, renewable appointment
eligibility: Master's or doctoral degree (or currently pursuing) in Computer Science, Engineering, Life/Health/Medical Sciences, Mathematics/Statistics, or Physics, received within the last 60 months; U.S. citizen or LPR
flags: US citizen/LPR required; must have lived in U.S. 36 of past 60 months
notes: One-year (renewable) ORISE fellowship analyzing large-scale medical device data to address AI/ML algorithm training, bias evaluation, and performance monitoring for FDA regulatory science.

organization: University of Virginia School of Education and Human Development (IES/NCER-funded)
name: VEST (Virginia Education Science Training) Predoctoral Two-Year Fellowship
amount: $34,000/year stipend plus up to $12,000/year toward tuition and health insurance
other_benefits: Interdisciplinary curriculum in causal inference and quantitative methods, faculty research mentorship, year-long apprenticeship with an education policy or practice organization, research/conference travel fund
eligibility: US citizens/permanent residents already admitted to a PhD program at UVA in Education Policy, Applied Developmental Science, Psychology, Sociology, or Economics
flags: Applicants must be admitted to/enrolled in a UVA doctoral program; US citizens/permanent residents only
notes: One of IES/NCER's longest-running predoctoral training grants (current award cycle runs through Aug 2026), training PhD students in causal-inference methods for rigorous, policy-relevant education research.

organization: University of Iowa College of Education (IES/NCER-funded)
name: IES Postdoctoral Training Program in Culturally Responsive Education Leadership & School Mental Health
amount: $60,000/year stipend with benefits, plus up to $10,000/year for research and professional development
other_benefits: Mentored research with an interdisciplinary faculty team, training in IES Standards for Excellence and advanced research methods, grant-writing and dissemination support
eligibility: Recent PhD/EdD holders in education leadership, education policy, or related fields with interest in culturally responsive, equity-oriented research
notes: IES/NCER-funded two-year postdoctoral fellowship (2022-2027 grant cycle, supporting 4 total fellows) focused on culturally responsive education leadership and school mental health research in rural and urban settings.

name: Harold Gulliksen Psychometric Research Fellowship
deadline: Annual — mid-January (recent cycle deadline was Jan 19)
amount: $25,000 stipend plus $8,000 toward tuition/fees, a research project grant, and $10,000 for required summer internship participation
other_benefits: Dissertation research mentorship from both the applicant's academic advisor and ETS mentors; includes participation in the Ida Lawrence Research Summer Internship
eligibility: Doctoral students at the dissertation stage in educational measurement, psychometrics, or statistics
notes: Longstanding ETS fellowship supporting dissertation-stage research in psychometrics and educational measurement, paired with a mentored ETS summer internship in Princeton, NJ.

organization: Weatherhead Center for International Affairs, Harvard University
name: Weatherhead Scholars Program Postdoctoral Fellowship
deadline: Annual — early November (e.g., Nov 4, 2025 for the 2026-27 cycle)
other_benefits: Office and library access at Harvard, visa sponsorship support for international scholars, participation in Weatherhead seminars and workshops
eligibility: PhD in a social science discipline (political science, sociology, economics, history, international affairs, etc.) earned within the past 3 years, or dissertation defended by appointment start; open to scholars of all nationalities
notes: One-year residential postdoctoral fellowship at Harvard for early-career social scientists working on international, comparative, or transnational issues; highly competitive (about 5% acceptance), with 5-10 fellows selected annually.

name: Minority Fellowship Program – Doctoral Fellowship in Mental Health and Substance Abuse Services (MHSAS-Doc)
deadline: Annual (2026/27 cohort closed; next cycle to be announced later in 2026)
other_benefits: Mentoring, leadership training, conference travel support, professional networking
eligibility: Full-time doctoral students in APA-accredited clinical, counseling, or school psychology programs committed to serving underserved communities; U.S. citizens, permanent residents, or noncitizen nationals
flags: U.S. citizens/permanent residents/noncitizen nationals only; SAMHSA-funded with priority for behavioral-health focus on underserved populations
notes: SAMHSA-funded doctoral fellowship for psychology students, renewable annually for up to 3 years; one program within APA's broader Minority Fellowship Program family.

organization: Society for the Psychological Study of Social Issues (SPSSI)
name: James Marshall Public Policy Fellowship
other_benefits: Full-time placement in a U.S. congressional office, conference costs covered, policy training delivered through the AAAS Science & Technology Policy Fellowships infrastructure
eligibility: Postdoctoral scientists (PhD/PsyD in hand) in fields relevant to the psychological study of social issues; SPSSI membership required
flags: SPSSI membership required
notes: One-year congressional placement (Sept 1-Aug 31) for early-career behavioral/social scientists to bridge psychological research and federal policymaking; a distinctly SPSSI-branded fellowship administered in coordination with AAAS.

organization: Jefferson Scholars Foundation (in partnership with the Miller Center, University of Virginia)
name: National Fellowship Program
amount: $30,000/year (renewable for a possible second year), plus up to $5,000 in additional research funds
other_benefits: Mentorship from established scholars, training workshops, required annual ~10-day residency in Charlottesville, professional network
eligibility: PhD candidates completing dissertations on U.S. politics, public policy, political economy, history, or sociology at leading institutions; open to non-U.S. citizens
notes: Dissertation-year fellowship (with possible second-year renewal) focused on U.S. politics and public policy research, pairing stipend support with mentorship from Miller Center-affiliated scholars.

name: CIP Research Fellowship
other_benefits: Remote research fellowship
eligibility: Researchers exploring AI, public values, and democratic governance
notes: Remote research fellowship exploring AI, public values, and democratic governance. Sourced from the private Fellowship Tracker.
